prompt: Create a 10-second Manim scene on a PolarPlane that draws a rose curve and labels the polar setup clearly.
completion:
from manim import *

class PolarRoseCurveDemo(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("A Polar Rose Curve", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        plane = PolarPlane(radius_max=3).add_coordinates()
        graph = plane.plot_polar_graph(lambda theta: 2 * np.sin(5 * theta), [0, TAU], color=ORANGE)
        caption = Text("Polar curves encode radius as a function of angle.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(plane), run_time=3)
        self.play(Create(graph), FadeIn(caption), run_time=4)
        self.wait(3)

prompt: Create a 10-second Manim scene showing stream lines in a simple outward flow field with a short caption explaining divergence.
completion:
from manim import *

class StreamlinesDivergenceDemo(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Stream Lines for an Outward Flow", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        stream_lines = StreamLines(
            lambda pos: 0.35 * pos + 0.1 * RIGHT,
            stroke_width=2,
            max_anchors_per_line=30,
            x_range=[-4, 4, 0.5],
            y_range=[-3, 3, 0.5],
        )
        caption = Text("Divergence means nearby trajectories spread apart.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), run_time=2)
        self.add(stream_lines)
        stream_lines.start_animation(warm_up=False, flow_speed=1.4)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption), run_time=2)
        self.wait(stream_lines.virtual_time / stream_lines.flow_speed)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 20-second ZoomedScene that zooms into a small pixel image, moves the zoom frame to a highlighted pixel block, and then pops the zoomed display back out.
completion:
from manim import *

class ZoomedPixelFocus(ZoomedScene):
    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(
            zoom_factor=0.35,
            zoomed_display_height=2.2,
            zoomed_display_width=4.2,
            image_frame_stroke_width=12,
            zoomed_camera_config={"default_frame_stroke_width": 3},
            **kwargs,
        )

    def construct(self):
        title = Text("ZoomedScene Is Useful for Tiny Motion Details", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        image = ImageMobject(np.uint8([
            [[15, 25, 120, 255], [30, 60, 160, 255], [180, 80, 40, 255], [230, 210, 60, 255]],
            [[40, 100, 180, 255], [60, 150, 210, 255], [210, 110, 70, 255], [240, 220, 110, 255]],
            [[80, 140, 70, 255], [100, 200, 100, 255], [220, 170, 90, 255], [250, 235, 150, 255]],
            [[120, 170, 90, 255], [150, 220, 140, 255], [235, 205, 120, 255], [255, 245, 180, 255]],
        ])).scale(1.6)
        focus = SurroundingRectangle(image, color=GRAY_C, buff=0.02)
        target_dot = Dot(image.get_center() + RIGHT * 0.55 + DOWN * 0.55, color=YELLOW, radius=0.05)
        frame = self.zoomed_camera.frame
        display = self.zoomed_display
        display.shift(DOWN * 2.1)
        frame.move_to(target_dot)
        frame.set_color(PURPLE)
        display.display_frame.set_color(RED_D)
        caption = Text("The zoom window can travel, expand, and then fold back in.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(image), Create(focus), FadeIn(target_dot), run_time=3)
        self.play(Create(frame), run_time=2)
        self.activate_zooming()
        self.play(self.get_zoomed_display_pop_out_animation(), run_time=3)
        self.play(frame.animate.shift(LEFT * 0.8 + UP * 0.8), run_time=3)
        self.play(display.animate.scale(1.2), FadeIn(caption), run_time=3)
        self.play(self.get_zoomed_display_pop_out_animation(), rate_func=lambda t: smooth(1 - t), run_time=3)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a short Manim scene on a NumberLine that marks an interval with two dots and uses BraceBetweenPoints to label the interval length.
completion:
from manim import *

class BraceIntervalMeasurement(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        line = NumberLine(x_range=[-1, 6, 1], include_numbers=True, length=8)
        left = Dot(line.n2p(1), color=BLUE_D)
        right = Dot(line.n2p(4), color=BLUE_D)
        brace = BraceBetweenPoints(left.get_bottom(), right.get_bottom(), DOWN)
        label = MathTex("3\ units", color=YELLOW_D).next_to(brace, DOWN)

        self.play(Create(line), FadeIn(left), FadeIn(right), run_time=2)
        self.play(GrowFromCenter(brace), FadeIn(label), run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 10-second Manim scene with a small MathTable of experiment results and highlight the best row with a caption.
completion:
from manim import *

class MathTableHighlight(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("A MathTable Can Summarize Model Results", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        table = MathTable(
            [["0.71", "0.62"], ["0.68", "0.75"], ["0.64", "0.81"]],
            row_labels=[Text("A"), Text("B"), Text("C")],
            col_labels=[Text("loss"), Text("score")],
            include_outer_lines=True,
        ).scale(0.75)
        highlight = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[3], color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.08)
        caption = Text("Row C trades lower loss for the strongest case score.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(table), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(highlight), FadeIn(caption), run_time=3)
        self.wait(3)

prompt: Create a 10-second Manim scene with a ParametricFunction, a dot moving along it, and a traced path captioned as a parametric trajectory.
completion:
from manim import *

class ParametricCurveProbe(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Parametric Curves Trace Position Over Time", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[-4, 4, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], x_length=8, y_length=5, tips=False)
        curve = ParametricFunction(lambda t: np.array([2.6 * np.cos(t), 1.5 * np.sin(2 * t), 0]), t_range=[0, TAU], color=PURPLE_D)
        dot = Dot(curve.get_start(), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08)
        trace = TracedPath(dot.get_center, stroke_color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=4)
        caption = Text("The parameter controls where the probe sits on the curve.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), Create(curve), run_time=4)
        self.add(trace)
        self.play(MoveAlongPath(dot, curve), FadeIn(dot), FadeIn(caption), rate_func=linear, run_time=4)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 10-second Manim scene on a NumberLine that marks a small discrete probability distribution with vertical lines, dots, and probability labels.
completion:
from manim import *

class ProbabilityNumberLineMasses(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Discrete Probability Mass on a Number Line", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        line = NumberLine(x_range=[0, 6, 1], include_numbers=True, length=8)
        xs = [1, 2, 4, 5]
        probs = [0.2, 0.35, 0.3, 0.15]
        stems = VGroup()
        labels = VGroup()
        dots = VGroup()
        for x, p in zip(xs, probs):
            base = line.n2p(x)
            top = base + UP * (2.8 * p + 0.3)
            stems.add(Line(base, top, color=BLUE_D, stroke_width=6))
            dots.add(Dot(top, color=YELLOW_D, radius=0.06))
            labels.add(MathTex(f"{p:.2f}", color=YELLOW_D).next_to(top, UP, buff=0.12))
        caption = Text("Each stem height encodes the probability mass at one value.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(line), run_time=3)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[Create(stem) for stem in stems], lag_ratio=0.15), LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(dot) for dot in dots], lag_ratio=0.15), LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(label) for label in labels], lag_ratio=0.15), FadeIn(caption), run_time=4)
        self.wait(3)

prompt: Create a 10-second Manim scene with a BarChart that first shows baseline values and then updates the bars to a second set of values with a caption about growth.
completion:
from manim import *

class BarGrowthWithUpdate(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Bars Can Update Over Time", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        chart = BarChart(
            values=[2, 3, 2.5],
            bar_names=["A", "B", "C"],
            y_range=[0, 6, 1],
            y_length=4.5,
            x_length=7,
        )
        chart.set_color_by_gradient(BLUE_D, TEAL_D)
        caption = Text("The same chart can animate from a baseline to a new state.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(chart), run_time=4)
        self.play(chart.animate.change_bar_values([3.5, 5.0, 4.2]), FadeIn(caption), run_time=3)
        self.wait(3)
